# How-To: CZ Gate Reports and Error Budgets

Gate-level Rydberg modeling is this package's flagship: two-qubit CZ
protocols, Nielsen average gate fidelity, and microscopic XYZ/AL/LG error
budgets — none of which has a Pulser equivalent.

## The gate namespace

```python
from ryd_gate.gates import (
    ARProtocol, DoubleARPProtocol, TOProtocol,      # the kernel protocol classes
    average_gate_infidelity, error_budget,           # the analysis functions
    CZGateReport, cz_gate_report,                    # the stable report surface
    optimize_cz_parameters,                          # parameter optimization
)
```

`ryd_gate.gates` re-exports kernel objects — it is a documented namespace,
not a second implementation.

## One-call gate report

```python
from ryd_gate import Register, RydbergSystem

X_TO_DARK = [-0.6894097925886826, 1.040962607910546, 0.3277877211544321,
             1.5639989822346387, 0.6689846026179691, 1.3407418093368753]

system = (RydbergSystem
          .set_atom_level("rb87_7", param_set="our", blackmanflag=True, detuning_sign=1)
          .set_atom_geom(Register.chain(2, spacing_um=3.0)).build())
report = cz_gate_report(system, TOProtocol(), X_TO_DARK, include_error_budget=False)

report.infidelity        # ~3.7e-7 (benchmark pin)
report.fidelity          # 1 - infidelity (derived, never stored)
report.phase_error_rad   # residual conditional-phase error, wrapped to [-pi, pi]
report.theta_rad         # the single-qubit Rz phase the protocol targets
report.residuals         # mean leakage populations: e1/e2/e3/ryd/ryd_garb
report.to_dict()         # "ryd-gate/cz-gate-report/v1"
```

The report evolves |00⟩, |01⟩, |11⟩ **once** through the same machinery as
`average_gate_infidelity` (shared overlap core); it never calls a backend
directly.

## Re-optimizing parameters

`optimize_cz_parameters` minimizes `average_gate_infidelity` with a
**theta-projection warm start**. Under the explicit |0⟩ model the single-qubit Z
correction (`theta = x[protocol.theta_index]`) is hyper-sensitive to the gate
time — |0⟩ sits at the 6.835 GHz clock splitting, so the optimal theta winds
rapidly with `T` and severely ill-conditions a *joint* search (a wrong theta
dominates the objective and masks the leakage / conditional-phase gradients). So
it re-fits theta alone first — a cheap 1-D projection onto the optimal-theta
ridge — then polishes all parameters:

```python
# a perturbative-era optimum whose single-qubit phase went stale under explicit |0⟩
x_seed = [-0.6989301339711643, 1.0296229082590798, 0.3759232324550267,
          1.5710180991068543, 1.4454279613697887, 1.3406239758422793]

result = optimize_cz_parameters(system, TOProtocol(), x_seed)
result.seed_infidelity    # ~2.2e-2  (seed: a real gate with a stale theta)
result.theta_infidelity   # ~2.4e-6  (after the 1-D theta re-fit alone)
result.infidelity         # ~3.7e-7  (after the full Nelder-Mead polish)
result.x                  # the optimized parameter vector
```

Pass `polish=False` to stop after the theta projection (e.g. to re-calibrate only
the single-qubit phase). Protocols without a single-qubit phase
(`theta_index is None`) skip the projection. Multi-start search **loops** live in
`scripts/` (`scripts/reoptimize_explicit.py`, `scripts/optimize_ar_cz.py`), which
call this once per start.

## Error budgets

With decay enabled at construction time, `error_budget` decomposes the
infidelity by physical source and decay channel class:

```python
system_decay = (RydbergSystem
                .set_atom_level("rb87_7", param_set="our", blackmanflag=True,
                                detuning_sign=1, enable_rydberg_decay=True)
                .set_atom_geom(Register.chain(2, spacing_um=3.0)).build())
report = cz_gate_report(system_decay, TOProtocol(), X_TO_DARK)
report.error_budget["rydberg_decay"]    # {"total": ..., "XYZ": ..., "AL": ..., "LG": ...}
```

## Benchmark status (pinned in tests/gates/test_cz_benchmark_pins.py)

- **TO dark** (`X_TO_DARK`, blackman flat-top, detuning_sign=+1): infidelity
  ≈ 3.7e-7 — the validated high-fidelity TO operating point.
- **AR re-optimized**: genuine high-fidelity amplitude-robust CZ gates on
  **both** parameter sets, found by `scripts/reoptimize_explicit.py` /
  `scripts/optimize_ar_cz.py`: `X_AR_OUR_OPT` → ≈ 7.2e-6 on `param_set="our"`,
  and `X_AR_LUKIN` → ≈ 1.6e-6 on `param_set="lukin"` (the lukin pin is
  `slow`-marked).
- The *legacy* `X_AR` parameters and the Saffman-constants Double-ARP
  configuration are **not** high-fidelity optima as written; their pins guard
  the computation path, not gate quality.

> **Why the legacy AR seed looks bad.** The legacy `X_AR` single-start gets
> trapped at infidelity ~0.45 on "our" — a *non-entangling local minimum*
> (low leakage, but conditional phase ≈ 0 instead of π), **not** a protocol or
> target bug: the TO gate reaches 3.7e-7 on the same system with the same
> `average_gate_infidelity`, and `scripts/diagnose_ar_target.py` confirms the
> 01↔10 symmetry is exact. A **multi-start** search (`--restarts`) escapes the
> local minimum; `optimize_cz_parameters` then makes each start effective via the
> theta projection. Use multi-start, not a single seed, when re-optimizing AR.

Noisy gate statistics combine this with the [NoiseModel](how_to_noise.qmd)
Monte Carlo runner.
